Title: Create a new report definition with a single SQL dataset
Preconditions: Logged in as user with Manage Reports privilege; Reporting module installed
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Manage Reports | 2. Create new report definition | 3. Add a SQL dataset definition | 4. Save the report definition
Test_Data: Report name=Active Patients; SQL=select patient_id from patient where voided=0
Expected_Result: Report definition saves and appears in the report definitions list with the SQL dataset attached

Title: Reject saving a report definition with a duplicate name
Preconditions: A report definition named "Active Patients" already exists
Test_Steps: 1. Create a new report definition | 2. Enter the name Active Patients | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: System rejects the save with a validation message indicating the name already exists; no duplicate is created

Title: Reject report definition with empty/blank name
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Reports privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Create a new report definition | 2. Leave the name field blank | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Validation error is shown; the report definition is not persisted

Title: Edit an existing report definition and verify version persistence
Preconditions: A saved report definition exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open an existing report definition | 2. Modify the SQL dataset | 3. Save | 4. Reopen the definition
Expected_Result: Edited SQL persists after reopen; prior runs are not retroactively altered

Title: Delete (retire) a report definition referenced by a schedule
Preconditions: A report definition is referenced by an active scheduled task
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to delete the report definition | 2. Observe handling of dependent schedule
Expected_Result: System either blocks deletion warning about the dependency or cascades and disables the schedule; no orphaned schedule executes

Title: Build a cohort by age range composition
Preconditions: Cohort Builder loaded; patient data present
Test_Steps: 1. Open Cohort Builder | 2. Add an Age search (min and max) | 3. Run the search | 4. Save the cohort
Expected_Result: Cohort returns only patients whose computed age falls within 18-65 inclusive; count matches source query

Title: Combine two cohorts with AND/OR/NOT boolean logic
Preconditions: Two named patient searches exist in the composition history
Test_Steps: 1. Run search A (gender=F) | 2. Run search B (age>=50) | 3. Compose 1 AND 2 | 4. Compose 1 OR 2 | 5. Compose 1 AND NOT 2
Test_Data: Compose expr: 1 AND 2; 1 OR 2; 1 AND NOT 2
Expected_Result: AND yields intersection, OR yields union, AND NOT yields set difference; counts are mathematically consistent (|A AND B| <= min, |A OR B| >= max)

Title: Boundary age values at exact min and max thresholds
Preconditions: Patients exist aged exactly 18 and exactly 65
Test_Steps: 1. Build Age search min=18 max=65 | 2. Run | 3. Inspect membership of the 18yo and 65yo patients
Test_Data: Age min=18; Age max=65; Patients aged 17,18,65,66
Expected_Result: Patients aged 18 and 65 are included; 17 and 66 are excluded; inclusive boundary behavior confirmed

Title: Invalid composition expression handling
Preconditions: At least one search in composition history
Test_Steps: 1. Enter composition expression referencing a nonexistent index | 2. Run
Expected_Result: System returns a clear error about the invalid reference and does not produce a misleading cohort

Title: Save and reload a named cohort definition
Preconditions: A cohort search has been run
Test_Steps: 1. Run a cohort search | 2. Save as named cohort definition | 3. Navigate away | 4. Reload the saved cohort | 5. Re-run
Expected_Result: Saved cohort transitions Draft->Saved->Loaded->Executed; re-run reproduces the same membership for unchanged data

Title: Empty cohort result handling
Preconditions: No patients match a deliberately impossible criterion
Test_Steps: 1. Build Age search min=200 max=210 | 2. Run
Expected_Result: Cohort returns zero members; UI shows an explicit empty-result state, not an error or blank crash

Title: Define a count indicator over a cohort
Preconditions: A cohort definition exists
Test_Steps: 1. Create a Cohort Indicator | 2. Map it to the saved cohort | 3. Save | 4. Add to a dataset and evaluate
Expected_Result: Indicator evaluates to the count of cohort members for the given parameter context

Title: Fraction/proportion indicator numerator and denominator
Preconditions: Numerator and denominator cohorts defined
Test_Steps: 1. Define indicator with numerator=on-treatment | 2. denominator=all-diagnosed | 3. Evaluate | 4. Verify percentage
Expected_Result: Indicator returns numerator, denominator and computed proportion; proportion equals num/den and is bounded 0-100%

Title: Divide-by-zero guard on proportion indicator
Preconditions: Denominator cohort yields zero members
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate a proportion indicator where denominator cohort is empty
Expected_Result: System returns a defined value (null/0/NA) without throwing an unhandled exception or infinity

Title: Indicator dimension disaggregation by gender
Preconditions: Indicator and a gender dimension defined
Test_Steps: 1. Attach a gender dimension to the indicator | 2. Evaluate | 3. Inspect Male/Female/Unknown columns
Expected_Result: Disaggregated values per gender sum to the total indicator value; no patient double-counted

Title: Evaluate report with date range parameters
Preconditions: A parameterized report definition exists
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report | 2. Supply start and end date parameters | 3. Execute
Expected_Result: Report includes only records within the inclusive date range; out-of-range records excluded

Title: Reject end date earlier than start date
Preconditions: Parameterized report with start/end dates
Test_Steps: 1. Run report | 2. Enter startDate after endDate | 3. Execute
Expected_Result: Validation prevents execution or returns an empty/defined result with a clear message; no misleading data

Title: Date boundary inclusivity at start and end of range
Preconditions: Records exist on exactly the start and end dates
Test_Steps: 1. Run report startDate=2026-01-01 endDate=2026-01-31 | 2. Verify records dated 2026-01-01 and 2026-01-31
Expected_Result: Records on the boundary dates are included; records one day outside are excluded

Title: Location parameter filters report by facility
Preconditions: Multiple locations exist with data
Test_Steps: 1. Run report | 2. Select a single location parameter | 3. Execute
Expected_Result: Only records associated with the selected location appear; other-location records excluded

Title: Pairwise combination of date range and location and gender parameters
Preconditions: Report with date, location, gender parameters
Test_Steps: 1. Execute report across pairwise combos of {Q1,Q2} x {ClinicA,ClinicB} x {M,F} | 2. Compare results
Expected_Result: Each parameter combination returns the correct filtered subset; no parameter is silently ignored

Title: Missing required parameter blocks execution
Preconditions: Report with a required date parameter
Test_Steps: 1. Run report | 2. Leave required startDate empty | 3. Execute
Expected_Result: Execution is blocked with a required-field validation; report does not run with a null parameter

Title: Malformed date parameter input rejected
Preconditions: Parameterized report
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a malformed date string | 2. Execute
Expected_Result: Input is rejected with a format validation error; no exception or zero-result masking

Title: Schedule a report to run on a daily cron
Preconditions: A report definition exists; scheduler service running
Test_Steps: 1. Open report scheduling | 2. Configure a daily schedule | 3. Save | 4. Wait for the scheduled time
Expected_Result: Report executes automatically at the scheduled time and a completed run record is created

Title: Scheduled report lifecycle state transitions
Preconditions: A scheduled report is configured
Test_Steps: 1. Observe states Scheduled->Running->Completed | 2. Trigger a failing run | 3. Observe Running->Failed | 4. Re-enable
Expected_Result: Run records correctly transition through Scheduled, Running, Completed, and Failed states; failed runs are flagged

Title: Pause and resume a scheduled report
Preconditions: An active scheduled report exists
Test_Steps: 1. Pause the schedule | 2. Confirm no run at next scheduled time | 3. Resume | 4. Confirm run at following scheduled time
Expected_Result: Paused schedule does not execute; resumed schedule executes at the next interval; transitions are audited

Title: Overlapping schedule runs do not corrupt output
Preconditions: A long-running report scheduled at a short interval
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule report whose runtime exceeds the interval | 2. Allow two windows to elapse
Expected_Result: Concurrent runs are queued or skipped per policy; outputs are not interleaved or corrupted

Title: Export report results to CSV
Preconditions: A report has produced result rows
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report | 2. Choose Export as CSV | 3. Download and open the file
Expected_Result: CSV downloads with correct header row, one row per record, and values matching the on-screen result

Title: Export report results to Excel (XLS/XLSX)
Preconditions: A report has produced result rows
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report | 2. Choose Export as Excel | 3. Open the workbook
Expected_Result: Excel file opens with correct sheet, headers and typed cells; numeric and date columns are not mangled

Title: Export report to PDF preserves layout
Preconditions: A report with a rendering template exists
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report | 2. Choose Export as PDF | 3. Open the PDF
Expected_Result: PDF renders with title, parameters, column headers and data; pagination is intact; no truncated columns

Title: CSV injection / formula prevention in export
Preconditions: A patient name field contains a leading equals sign
Test_Steps: 1. Create patient with name starting with =cmd | 2. Run report including names | 3. Export CSV | 4. Inspect cell
Expected_Result: Dangerous leading characters are escaped or sanitized so spreadsheet apps do not execute formulas on open

Title: Special characters and unicode preserved in CSV
Preconditions: Records contain commas, quotes, and non-ASCII names
Test_Steps: 1. Run report with names containing , " and accents | 2. Export CSV | 3. Re-import/parse
Expected_Result: Fields with delimiters/quotes are properly quoted and escaped; unicode is UTF-8 preserved; round-trip parse succeeds

Title: Export of empty result set produces header-only file
Preconditions: A report that yields zero rows
Test_Steps: 1. Run report with no matching data | 2. Export CSV
Expected_Result: CSV contains the header row and no data rows; export does not error

Title: Export of a large result set completes without timeout
Preconditions: A report returning 100k+ rows is available
Test_Steps: 1. Run a high-volume report | 2. Export CSV | 3. Verify row count and completion
Expected_Result: Export streams to completion within SLA, file row count equals result count, server memory stays bounded

Title: Report evaluation performance under large cohort
Preconditions: Database seeded with a large patient population
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate an indicator over a 500k-patient cohort | 2. Measure response time
Expected_Result: Evaluation completes within the documented performance SLA; no out-of-memory error

Title: Result grid paginates large output correctly
Preconditions: A report returns more rows than one page
Test_Steps: 1. Run report returning >200 rows | 2. Navigate next/previous pages | 3. Jump to last page
Expected_Result: Pages show correct contiguous slices; total count is accurate; last page shows remainder (37 rows)

Title: Page boundary off-by-one at exact page size multiple
Preconditions: Result count is an exact multiple of page size
Test_Steps: 1. Run report returning exactly 100 rows with page size 50 | 2. Navigate to page 2 | 3. Attempt page 3
Expected_Result: Exactly two pages exist; page 3 is unavailable; no empty trailing page is rendered

Title: Sort stability across paginated pages
Preconditions: A sortable multi-page result
Test_Steps: 1. Sort by a column | 2. Page through all pages | 3. Verify ordering continuity
Expected_Result: Sort order is consistent across page boundaries; no duplicate or skipped records between pages

Title: Unprivileged user cannot run restricted report
Preconditions: User without Run Reports privilege logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as restricted user | 2. Attempt to access Manage/Run Reports
Expected_Result: Access is denied; report menu/endpoint returns an authorization error; no report data is exposed

Title: Direct URL access to report run is authorization-checked
Preconditions: Restricted user with a known report run URL
Test_Steps: 1. Capture an authorized report URL | 2. Log in as restricted user | 3. Request the URL directly
Expected_Result: Server enforces authorization on the endpoint, not just the UI; request is rejected with 403

Title: Location-scoped user sees only permitted location data
Preconditions: User restricted to a single login location
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as location-scoped user | 2. Run a multi-location report
Expected_Result: Report results are limited to the user's permitted location; other locations' PHI is not returned

Title: Report execution is recorded in the audit log
Preconditions: Audit/logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Run a report as an identified user | 2. Inspect the audit log
Expected_Result: Audit entry records the user, report, parameters, and timestamp of execution for accountability

Title: Report count matches direct database query
Preconditions: DB access available for verification
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report and note the count | 2. Execute the equivalent SQL against the database | 3. Compare
Test_Data: SQL=select count(*) from patient where voided=0
Expected_Result: Report count exactly equals the direct database count; no off-by-one or voided-record leakage

Title: Voided/retired records excluded from results
Preconditions: Some patients and obs are voided
Test_Steps: 1. Void a known patient | 2. Re-run the report | 3. Confirm exclusion
Expected_Result: Voided patients and retired records are excluded from report results and counts

Title: Aggregate sum equals sum of detail rows
Preconditions: A report with both detail and summary sections
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report | 2. Sum the detail numeric column | 3. Compare to the summary total
Expected_Result: Summary total equals the arithmetic sum of detail rows; no rounding drift beyond defined precision

Title: Timezone handling for datetime fields in report
Preconditions: Encounters recorded near midnight in server timezone
Test_Steps: 1. Record an encounter at 23:30 local | 2. Run a date-bounded report | 3. Verify the encounter date bucketing
Expected_Result: Datetime is bucketed in the correct day per the report timezone policy; no off-by-one-day from UTC conversion

Title: Indicator over empty cohort returns zero not null-crash
Preconditions: An indicator mapped to a cohort with no members
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate the indicator | 2. Inspect rendered value
Expected_Result: Indicator renders 0 (or defined empty value) cleanly; report does not error or show blank cell ambiguity

Title: Report with no data shows explicit empty-state message
Preconditions: A report guaranteed to return no rows
Test_Steps: 1. Run the report | 2. Observe the UI
Expected_Result: UI displays an explicit "No data for selected criteria" message rather than a blank or broken grid

Title: Explore report behavior with extreme parameter combinations
Preconditions: Tester familiar with reporting module
Test_Steps: 1. Try wide date ranges, null locations, max page sizes, rapid re-runs | 2. Observe stability and correctness
Expected_Result: No crashes, data leaks, or silent wrong results surface; anomalies are documented with repro steps

Title: Report results grid is keyboard navigable and screen-reader friendly
Preconditions: Report results displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the grid using keyboard only | 2. Verify table headers, captions and ARIA roles with a screen reader
Expected_Result: Table has programmatic header associations, focus order is logical, and pagination controls are operable by keyboard (WCAG 2.1 AA)

Title: Export controls have accessible labels
Preconditions: Export buttons present
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to each export control | 2. Verify accessible name announced
Expected_Result: Each export control exposes a descriptive accessible name; no icon-only unlabeled buttons

Title: SQL dataset definition is protected against injection via parameters
Preconditions: A SQL-backed report with a string parameter
Test_Steps: 1. Supply a parameter value containing a SQL meta-payload | 2. Execute
Expected_Result: Parameter is bound/parameterized; injection does not alter the query; no extra rows or errors returned

Title: Exported file does not leak data beyond user authorization scope
Preconditions: Location-scoped analyst
Test_Steps: 1. Run and export a report as a scoped user | 2. Inspect file contents
Expected_Result: Exported file contains only data the user is authorized to see; no cross-tenant or cross-location PHI leakage

Title: Run a report via REST and retrieve results
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid session/token
Test_Steps: 1. POST to run the report with parameters | 2. Poll status | 3. GET results
Expected_Result: API accepts parameters, returns a request id, status transitions to COMPLETED, and results are retrievable as JSON

Title: Unauthenticated report API request is rejected
Preconditions: No session/token supplied
Test_Steps: 1. Call the report REST endpoint without credentials
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized; no report data is returned

Title: Report API rejects invalid parameter payload
Preconditions: Authenticated API client
Test_Steps: 1. POST report request with a malformed parameter type | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API responds with 4xx and a descriptive validation error; no 500 server fault

Title: Retrieve an aggregate via FHIR Measure/MeasureReport
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint supports Measure evaluation
Test_Steps: 1. Define/identify a Measure | 2. Invoke $evaluate-measure with period | 3. Inspect MeasureReport
Expected_Result: A MeasureReport resource is returned with population counts (numerator/denominator) and a valid measure-score

Title: FHIR MeasureReport population counts reconcile with source
Preconditions: A Measure evaluable against known data
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate the measure | 2. Compare numerator/denominator to source query counts
Expected_Result: MeasureReport numerator and denominator equal the independently computed source counts; group codes are correct

Title: Bulk Data export ($export) produces NDJSON for analytics
Preconditions: FHIR Bulk Data ($export) supported
Test_Steps: 1. Kick off a system or group $export | 2. Poll the status endpoint | 3. Download NDJSON files
Expected_Result: Async export returns 202 with a polling location, completes, and yields valid NDJSON files per resource type

Title: ORU result feed populates analytics consistently
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface processing ORU messages
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 with a numeric result | 2. Run a result-based indicator | 3. Verify inclusion
Expected_Result: The HL7-delivered observation is reflected in the analytics result with correct value and units

Title: Malformed HL7 message does not corrupt aggregate counts
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface
Test_Steps: 1. Send a malformed/duplicate ORU message | 2. Re-run the dependent indicator
Expected_Result: Duplicate/invalid messages are rejected or deduplicated; indicator counts are not double-incremented

Title: Pairwise indicator x dimension x period evaluation
Preconditions: Indicator with multiple dimensions and periods
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate across pairwise combos of {count,proportion} x {gender,ageGroup} x {monthly,quarterly}
Expected_Result: Each combination evaluates correctly and disaggregated cells reconcile to totals; no combination errors out

Title: Scheduled export delivered to configured destination
Preconditions: Schedule configured with a file/email destination
Test_Steps: 1. Configure scheduled report with delivery destination | 2. Let it run | 3. Verify delivery
Expected_Result: Generated output is delivered to the configured destination with correct file and no PHI sent to wrong recipient

Title: Concurrent edits to source data reflect in next run not prior export
Preconditions: A completed report run/export exists
Test_Steps: 1. Note a completed run | 2. Modify underlying source data | 3. Re-run | 4. Compare old export vs new run
Expected_Result: Prior export remains immutable snapshot; new run reflects the updated data; counts differ by exactly the change

Title: Pairwise patient attribute search combinations
Preconditions: Cohort builder with attribute search support
Test_Steps: 1. Build searches across pairwise combos of {gender M/F} x {age <18,>=18} x {hasActiveVisit yes/no} | 2. Run each
Expected_Result: Each attribute combination returns a correct, non-overlapping-where-expected cohort; counts are internally consistent
